description: Seed concept map for reverse tracing Korean translations of classical texts. Extend locally for project needs.

concepts.notes: Freedom/liberty vocabulary differs sharply by author and period.
Useful for Rousseau-like passages and political slavery metaphors.
Do not flatten commonwealth, state, civitas, polis, and res publica.
For German philosophy, distinguish Vernunft from Verstand.
Greek arete may mean excellence more broadly than moral virtue.
Eudaimonia should not be treated as simple emotion happiness.
Separate law, right, nature, and state-of-nature traditions.
Hobbes covenant and Rousseau contrat social are not interchangeable.

en: war of every man against every man
warre of every man against every man
concepts.notes: Hobbesian state of nature vocabulary; exact Leviathan wording may use early modern spelling.

en: solitary poor nasty brutish and short
fr: solitaire pauvre mechante brutale et courte
de: einsam arm ekelhaft tierisch und kurz
concepts.notes: Famous Hobbes passage from Leviathan Chapter 13; translations vary widely.

en: covenants without the sword are but words
concepts.notes: Famous Hobbes passage from Leviathan Chapter 17.
Useful for Stoic, Spinozist, and theological contexts.
